pages.title: General 2 — Carolyn Harris MP
headings: h2: My journey since becoming a Member of Parliament
pages.text_excerpt: General 2 — Carolyn Harris MP 0 Skip to Content Home Contact About Me Everyone Deserves Carolyn Harris MP Open Menu Close Menu Home Contact About Me Everyone Deserves Carolyn Harris MP Open Menu Close Menu Home Contact About Me Everyone Deserves My journey since becoming a Member of Parliament Born and raised in South Wales, it is an honour to represent the community I grew up in. Since being elected MP for Swansea East in 2015, I have had a proud career as an all-action campaigner. In Parliament, I have successfully worked to lower the stakes on fixed-odds betting terminals (FOBTs) and created a children’s funeral fund for bereaved families in memory of my son Martin, who passed away at eight years old. Currently, I champion the menopause revolution, seeking greater support for women experiencing symptoms and better awareness of menopause. Recently, I have been campaigning for menopause to be included in NHS health checks, arguing that it would be cost-neutral and could prevent unnecessary GP visits. I also continue to push for workplace policies that support women experiencing menopause, ensuring they receive fair treatment and opportunities to thrive in their careers. Locally, I have spearheaded the "Everyone Deserves" campaign, which provides hampers and cooked meals to families in need across South Wales during the festive seasons and summer holidays. Last year, we delivered around 1,500 hampers to families at Christmas, and with the support of local businesses and volunteers, we aim to reach even more this year. Following the 2024 boundary changes in the UK, I now represent the newly formed constituency of Neath and Swansea East. Since being elected I am focused on getting to know the new areas within the constituency and building relationships with the community. I am committed to being more than just a representative in Parliament; I aim to be a friend and a partner to every resident. I will work hand in hand with you to rebuild and strengthen our communities, ensuring that Neath and Swansea East becomes an even better place for all of us. Campaigns Everyone Deserves The Everyone Deserves campaign has been a huge success, helping to relieve pressure on food banks and ensuring that the community is well-fed during the holidays. Learn More Menopause This campaign involves improving menopause awareness, advocating for better healthcare, workplace support, and breaking the stigma surrounding menopause. Learn More Gambling Related Harm Gambling related harm was set up to tackle gambling addiction, pushing for stricter regulations and better support for those affected. pages.title: Latest News 1 — Carolyn Harris MP
pages.h1: MP calls for wider mounjaro access on NHS
pages.text_excerpt: Latest News 1 — Carolyn Harris MP 0 Skip to Content Home Contact About Me Everyone Deserves Carolyn Harris MP Open Menu Close Menu Home Contact About Me Everyone Deserves Carolyn Harris MP Open Menu Close Menu Home Contact About Me Everyone Deserves MP calls for wider mounjaro access on NHS January 7th 2026 On January 7th, I appeared on Good Morning Britain to speak about my experience with weight-loss medication and why access should not be based on BMI alone. For many people, weight affects far more than appearance. It can impact mobility, chronic pain, surgery waiting lists and overall quality of life. These broader health factors matter and should be considered when making decisions. I have tried many different approaches over the years, including various diets and weight-loss programmes. What finally worked for me helped me take back control of my health, alongside medical support and lifestyle changes. However, it is not easily affordable or accessible for everyone. Prevention is vital. We must continue to improve access to healthy food, support physical activity and tackle the impact of ultra-processed foods. But prevention alone does not help everyone who is already living with weight-related health conditions. Weight-loss medication should be considered as one part of a broader, medically supported approach to health, with strong safeguards and proper clinical oversight. It should not be treated as a lifestyle choice. I also spoke about this issue in an article for BBC News, where I outlined why we need a system that considers the whole person, rather than relying on a single measure to determine access to support. This should never be about one solution alone. We need prevention, support and treatment working together, so people can access the right help at the right time.
